   131                                  ;; Zeropage
   132                          C64_D6510	= $00	;Data Direction register
   133                          C64_R6510	= $01	;Data buffer
   134                          			;$02 Unused
   135                          C64_ADRAY1	= $03	;-$04 Jump Vector: Convert Integer--Floating
   136                          C64_ADRAY2	= $05	;-$06 Jump Vector: Convert Integer--Floating
   137                          C64_CHARAC	= $07	;Search Character
   138                          C64_ENDCHR	= $08	;Flag: Scan for Quote at End of String
   139                          C64_TRMPOS	= $09	;Screen Column From Last TAB
   140                          C64_VERCK	= $0A	;Flag: 0 = Load, 1 = Verify
   141                          C64_COUNT	= $0B	;Input Buffer Pointer / No. of Subscripts
   142                          C64_DIMFLG	= $0C	;Flag: Default Array DiMension
   143                          C64_VAUYP	= $0D	;Data Type: $FF = String, $00 = Numeric
   144                          C64_INTFLG	= $0E	;Data Type: $80 = Integer, $00 = Floating
   145                          C64_GARBFL	= $0F	;Flag: DATA scan/LlST quote/Garbage Coll
   146                          C64_SUBFLG	= $10	;Flag: Subscript Ref / User Function Call
   147                          C64_INPFLG	= $11	;Flag: $00 = INPUT, $40 = GET, $98 = READ
   148                          C64_TANSGN	= $12	;Flag TAN sign / Comparison Result
   149                          C64_CHANNL	= $13	;Current I/O Channel (CMD Logical File) Number
   150                          C64_LINNUM	= $14	;-$15 Temp: Integer Value
   151                          C64_TEMPPT	= $16	;Pointer Temporary String
   152                          C64_LASTPT	= $17	;-$18 Last Temp String Address
   153                          C64_TEMPST	= $19	;-$21 Stack for Temporary Strings
   154                          C64_INDEX	= $22	;Utility Pointer Area
   155                          C64_INDEX1	= $22	;-$23 First Utility Pointer.
   156                          C64_INDEX2	= $24	;-$25 Second Utility Pointer.
   157                          C64_RESHO	= $26	;-$2A Floating-Point Product of Multiply
   158                          C64_TXTTAB	= $2B	;-$2C Pointer: Start of BASIC Text
   159                          C64_VARTAB	= $2D	;-$2E Pointer: Start of BASIC Variables
   160                          C64_ARYTAB	= $2F	;-$30 Pointer: Start of BASIC Arrays
   161                          C64_STREND	= $31	;-$32 Pointer: End of BASIC Arrays (+1)
   162                          C64_FRETOP	= $33	;-$34 Pointer: Bottom of String Storage
   163                          C64_FRESPC	= $35	;-$36 Utility String Pointer
   164                          C64_BASSIZ	= $37	;-$38 Pointer: Highest Address Used by BASIC
   165                          C64_CURLIN	= $39	;-$3A Current BASIC Line Number
   166                          C64_OLDLIN	= $3B	;-$3C Previous BASIC Line Number
   167                          C64_OLDTXT	= $3D	;-$3E Pointer: BASIC Statement for CONT
   168                          C64_DATLIN	= $3F	;-$40 Current DATA Line Number
   169                          C64_DATPTR	= $41	;-$42 Pointer: Current DATA Item Address
   170                          C64_INPPTR	= $43	;-$44 Vector: INPUT Routine 
   171                          C64_VARNAM	= $45	;-$46 Current BASIC Variable Name
   172                          C64_VARPNT	= $47	;-$48 Pointer: Current BASIC Variable Data(FOUT uses it)
   173                          C64_FORPNT	= $49	;-$4A Pointer: Index Variable for FOR/NEXT
   174                          C64_VARTXT	= $4B	;-$4C Temp storage for TXTPTR during READ, INPUT, GET.
   175                          C64_OPMASK	= $4D	;Mask used during FRMEVL.
   176                          C64_TEMPF3	= $4E	;-$52 Temporary storage for FLPT value.
   177                          C64_FOUR6	= $53	;Length of String Variable during Garbege collection.
   178                          C64_JMPER	= $54	;-$56 JMP used in fun Eval - followed by fun address
   179                          C64_TEMPF0	= $56	;used by FLPT addition, subtraction and exp routines.
   180                          C64_TEMPF1	= $57	;-$5B Temporary storage for FLPT value.
   181                          C64_TEMPF2	= $5C	;-$60 Temporary storage for FLPT value.
   182                          C64_FAC    	= $61   ;FP Accumulator (6 bytes: 1 exp, 4 mantissa, 1 sign)
   183                          C64_FACEXP	= $61	;Floating-Point Accumulator #1: Exponent
   184                          C64_FACHO	= $62	;-$65 Floating Accum. #1: Mantissa
   185                          C64_FACSGN	= $66	;Floating Accum. #1: Sign
   186                          C64_SGNFLG	= $67	;Pointer: Series Evaluation Constant
   187                          C64_BITS	= $68	;Floating Accum. #1: Overflow Digit
   188                          C64_ARG    	= $69   ;FP Argument (6 bytes: 1 exp, 4 mantissa, 1 sign)
   189                          C64_ARGEXP	= $69	;Floating-Point Accumulator #2: Exponent
   190                          C64_ARGHO	= $6A	;-$6D Floating Accum. #2: Mantissa
   191                          C64_ARGSGN	= $6E	;Floating Accum. #2: Sign
   192                          C64_ARISGN	= $6F	;Sign Comparison Result: Accum. #1 vs #2
   193                          C64_FACRND 	= $70	;Floating Accum. #1. Low-Order (Rounding)
   194                          C64_FBUFPT	= $71	;-$72 Pointer: Cassette Buffer
   195                          C64_CHRGET	= $73	;-$8A Subroutine: Get Next Byte of BASIC Text
   196                          C64_CHRGOT	= $79	;Entry to Get Same Byte of Text Again
   197                          C64_CHRPTR 	= $7A   ;Ptr to current byte in BASIC prg or direct command.
   198                          C64_RNDX	= $8B	;-$8F Floating RND Function Seed Value
   199                          C64_STATUS	= $90	;Kernal I/O Status Word: ST
   200                          C64_STKEY	= $91	;Flag: STOP key / RVS key
   201                          C64_SVXT	= $92	;Timing Constant for Tape
   202                          C64_VERCK2	= $93	;Flag: 0 = Load, 1 = Verify
   203                          C64_C3PO	= $94	;Flag: Serial Bus-Output Char. Buffered
   204                          C64_BSOUR	= $95	;Buffered Character for Serial Bus
   205                          C64_SYNO	= $96	;Cassette Sync No.
   206                          C64_XSAV	= $97	;Temp Data Area
   207                          C64_LDTND	= $98	;No. of Open Files / Index to File Table
   208                          C64_DFLTN	= $99	;Default Input Device (0)
   209                          C64_DFLTO	= $9A	;Default Output (CMD) Device (3)
   210                          C64_PRTY	= $9B	;Tape Character Parity
   211                          C64_PSW 	= $9C	;Flag: Tape Byte-Received
   212                          C64_MSGFLG	= $9D	;Flag: $80 = Direct Mode, $00 = Program
   213                          C64_PTR1	= $9E	;Tape Pass 1 Error Log
   214                          C64_PTR2	= $9F	;Tape Pass 2 Error Log
   215                          C64_TIME	= $A0	;-$A2 Real-Time Jiffy Clock (approx) 1/60 Sec
   216                          C64_R2D2_PCNTR	= $A3	;Serial Bus EOI/Bit Counter Tape Read or Write
   217                          C64_BSOUR1_FIRT	= $A4	;Serial Bus shift Counter/Pulse Counter Tape Read
   218                          C64_CNTDN	= $A5	;Cassette Sync Countdown
   219                          C64_BUFPNT	= $A6	;Pointer: Tape I/O Buffer
   220                          C64_INBIT	= $A7	;RS-232 Input Bits / Cassette Temp
   221                          C64_BITCI	= $A8	;RS-232 Input Bit Count / Cassette Temp

   223                          C64_RIDATA	= $AA	;RS-232 Input Byte Buffer/Cassette Temp
   224                          C64_RIPRTY	= $AB	;RS-232 Input Parity / Cassette Short Cnt
   225                          C64_SAL 	= $AC	;-$AD Pointer: Tape Buffer/ Screen Scrolling
   226                          C64_EAL 	= $AE	;-$AF Tape End Addresses/End of Program
   227                          C64_CMP0	= $B0	;-$B1 Tape Timing Constants
   228                          C64_TAPE1	= $B2	;-$B3 Pointer: Start of Tape Buffer
   229                          C64_BITTS	= $B4	;RS-232 Out Bit Count / Cassette Temp
   230                          C64_NXTBIT	= $B5	;RS-232 Next Bit to Send/ Tape EOT Flag
   231                          C64_RODATA	= $B6	;RS-232 Out Byte Buffer
   232                          C64_FNLEN	= $B7	;Length of Current File Name
   233                          C64_LA  	= $B8	;-$B9 Current Logical File Number
   234                          C64_FA  	= $BA	;Current Device Number
   235                          C64_FNADR	= $BB	;-$BC Pointer: Current File Name
   236                          C64_ROPRTY	= $BD	;RS-232 Out Parity / Cassette Temp
   237                          C64_FSBLK	= $BE	;Cassette Read / Write Block Count
   238                          C64_MYCH	= $BF	;Serial Word Buffer
   239                          C64_CAS1	= $C0	;Tape Motor Interlock
   240                          C64_STAL	= $C1	;-$C2 I/O Start Address
   241                          C64_MEMUSS	= $C3	;-$C4 Tape Load Temps
   242                          C64_LSTX	= $C5	;Current Key Pressed: CHR$(n)  No Key
   243                          C64_NDX 	= $C6	;No. of Chars. in Keyboard Buffer (Queue)
   244                          C64_RVS 	= $C7	;Flag: Print Reverse Chars. -1=Yes, 0=No Used
   245                          C64_INDX	= $C8	;Pointer: End of Logical Line for INPUT
   246                          C64_LXSP	= $C9	;-$CA Cursor X-Y Pos. at Start of INPUT
   247                          C64_SFDX	= $CB	;Flag: Print Shifted Chars.
   248                          C64_BLNSW	= $CC	;Cursor Blink enable: 0 = Flash Cursor
   249                          C64_BLNCT	= $CD	;Timer: Countdown to Toggle Cursor
   250                          C64_GDBLN	= $CE	;Character Under Cursor
   251                          C64_BLNON	= $CF	;Flag: Last Cursor Blink On/Off
   252                          C64_CRSW	= $D0	;Flag: INPUT or GET from Keyboard
   253                          C64_PNT 	= $D1	;-$D2 Pointer: Current Screen Line Address
   254                          C64_PNTR	= $D3	;Cursor Column on Current Line
   255                          C64_QTSW	= $D4	;Flag: Editor in Quote Mode, $00 = NO
   256                          C64_LNMX	= $D5	;Physical Screen Line Length
   257                          C64_TBLX	= $D6	;-$D7 Current Cursor Physical Line Number
   258                          C64_INSRT	= $D8	;Flag: Insert Mode, >0 = # INSTs
   259                          C64_LDTB1	= $D9	;-$F2 Screen Line Link Table / Editor Temps
   260                          C64_USER	= $F3	;-$F4 Pointer: Current Screen Color RAM loc.
   261                          C64_KEYTAB	= $F5	;-$F6 Vector Keyboard Decode Table
   262                          C64_RIBUF	= $F7	;-$F8 RS-232 Input Buffer Pointer
   263                          C64_ROBUF	= $F9	;-$FA RS-232 Output Buffer Pointer
   264                          C64_FREKZP	= $FB	;-$FE Free 0-Page Space for User Programs
   265                          C64_BASZPT	= $FF	;BASIC Temp Data Area

   267                                  ;; Other BASIC/Kernal variables
   268                          C64_BAD 	= $0100	;Tape Input Error Log
   269                          C64_BUF 	= $0200	;System INPUT Buffer
   270                          C64_LAT 	= $0259	;Kernal Table: Active Logical File No's.
   271                          C64_FAT 	= $0263	;Kernal Table: Device No. for Each File
   272                          C64_SAT 	= $026D	;Kernal Table: Second Address Each File

   274                          C64_MEMSTR	= $0281	;Pointer: Bottom of Memory for O.S.
   275                          C64_MEMSIZ	= $0283	;Pointer: Top of Memory for O.S.
   276                          C64_TIMOUT	= $0285	;Flag: Kernal Variable for IEEE Timeout
   277                          C64_COLOR	= $0286	;Current Character Color Code
   278                          C64_GDCOL	= $0287	;Background Color Under Cursor
   279                          C64_HIBASE	= $0288	;Top of Screen Memory (Page)
   280                          C64_XMAX	= $0289	;Size of Keyboard Buffer
   281                          C64_RPTFLG	= $028A	;Flag: REPEAT Key Used, $80 = Repeat
   282                          C64_KOUNT	= $028B	;Repeat Speed Counter
   283                          C64_DELAY	= $028C	;Repeat Delay Counter
   284                          C64_SHFLAG	= $028D	;Flag: Keyb'rd SHIFT Key/CTRL Key/C= Key
   285                          C64_LSTSHF	= $028E	;Last Keyboard Shift Pattern
   286                          C64_KEYLOG	= $028F	;Vector: Keyboard Table Setup
   287                          C64_MODE	= $0291	;Flag: $00=Disable SHIFT Keys, $80 = Enable SHIFT Keys
   288                          C64_AUTODN	= $0292	;Flag: Auto Scroll Down, 0 = ON
   289                          C64_M51CTR	= $0293	;RS-232: 6551 Control Register Image
   290                          C64_MS1CDR	= $0294	;RS-232: 6551 Command Register Image
   291                          C64_M51AJB	= $0295	;RS-232 Non-Standard BPS (Time/2-100) USA
   292                          C64_RSSTAT	= $0297	;RS-232: 6551 Status Register Image
   293                          C64_BITNUM	= $0298	;RS-232 Number of Bits Left to Send
   294                          C64_BAUDOF	= $0299	;RS-232 Baud Rate: Full Bit Time (us)
   295                          C64_RIDBE	= $029B	;RS-232 Index to End of Input Buffer
   296                          C64_RIDBS	= $029C	;RS-232 Start of Input Buffer (Page)
   297                          C64_RODBS	= $029D	;RS-232 Start of Output Buffer (Page)
   298                          C64_RODBE	= $029E	;RS-232 Index to End of Output Buffer
   299                          C64_IRQTMP	= $029F	;Holds IRQ Vector During Tape I/O
   300                          C64_ENABL	= $02A1	;RS-232 Enables
   301                          C64_CASTON	= $02A2	;TOD Sense During Cassette I/O
   302                          C64_KIKA26	= $02A3	;Temp Storage For Cassette Read
   303                          C64_STUPID	= $02A4	;Temp D1 IRQ Indicator For Cassette Read
   304                          C64_LINTMP	= $02A5	;Temp For Line Index
   305                          C64_PALNTS	= $02A6	;PAL/NTSC Flag, O= NTSC, 1 = PAL
   306                          
   307                          	;; Vectors for some BASIC and Kernal routines
   308                          C64_IERROR 	= $0300 ;Vector: BASIC Print Error Mesg Routine (default $E38B)
   309                          C64_IMAIN  	= $0302 ;Vector: BASIC Program loop (default $A483)
   310                          C64_ICRNCH 	= $0304 ;Vector: BASIC CRUNCH Routine (default $A57C)
   311                          C64_IQPLOP 	= $0306 ;Vector: BASIC QPLOP Routine (default $A71A)
   312                          C64_IGONE  	= $0308 ;Vector: BASIC GONE Routine (default $A7E4)
   313                          C64_IEVAL  	= $030A ;Vector: BASIC EVAL Routine (default $AE86)
   314                          C64_SAREG  	= $030C ;Storage Area for SYS -- Accumulator
   315                          C64_SXREG  	= $030D ;Storage Area for SYS -- X Register
   316                          C64_SYREG  	= $030E ;Storage Area for SYS -- Y Register
   317                          C64_SPREG  	= $030F ;Storage Area for SYS -- Processor Status
   318                          C64_USRPOK 	= $0310 ;JMP Instruction for USR command ($4C)
   319                          C64_USRADD 	= $0311 ;Address of USR routine (default: $B248, FCERR)
   320                          C64_UNUSED 	= $0313 ;UNUSED
   321                          C64_CINV   	= $0314 ;Vector: Kernal IRQ Routine (default $EA31)  
   322                          C64_CBINV  	= $0316 ;Vector: Kernal BRK Routine (default $FE66)  
   323                          C64_NMINV  	= $0318 ;Vector: Kernal NMI Routine (default $FE47)  
   324                          C64_IOPEN  	= $031A ;Vector: Kernal OPEN Routine (default $F34A)  
   325                          C64_ICLOSE 	= $031C ;Vector: Kernal CLOSE Routine (default $F291)  
   326                          C64_ICHKIN 	= $031E ;Vector: Kernal CHKIN Routine (default $F20E)  
   327                          C64_ICHKOUT	= $0320 ;Vector: Kernal CHKOUT Routine (default $F250)
   328                          C64_ICLRCHN	= $0322 ;Vector: Kernal CLRCHN Routine (default $F333)  
   329                          C64_ICHRIN 	= $0324 ;Vector: Kernal CHRIN Routine (default $F157)  
   330                          C64_ICHROUT	= $0326 ;Vector: Kernal CHROUT Routine (default $F1CA)  
   331                          C64_ISTOP  	= $0328 ;Vector: Kernal STOP Routine (default $F6ED)
   332                          C64_IGETIN 	= $032A ;Vector: Kernal GETIN Routine (default $F13E)  
   333                          C64_ICLALL 	= $032C ;Vector: Kernal CLALL Routine (default $F32F)  
   334                          C64_USRCMD 	= $032E ;Vector: BASIC User-Defined Command (default $FE66)
   335                          C64_ILOAD  	= $0330 ;Vector: Kernal LOAD Routine (default $F49E)  
   336                          C64_ISAVE  	= $0332 ;Vector: Kernal SAVE Routine (default $F5DD)
   337                          C64_TBUFFR	= $033C	;Tape I/O Buffer 
   338                          
   339                          	;; Default address space
   340                          C64_VICSCN	= $0400	;1024 Byte Screen Memory Area
   341                          C64_BASRAM 	= $0800 ;Start address of BASIC RAM - should be $00
   342                          C64_BASROM 	= $A000 ;Start address of BASIC ROM (Vector: BASIC cold start)
   343                          C64_HIRAM  	= $C000 ;Start address of free high RAM area
   344                          C64_IOROM  	= $D000 ;Start address of I/O ROM
   345                          C64_KERROM 	= $E000 ;Start address of Kernal ROM
   346                          		
   347                                  ;; FLOATING POINT ROUTINES
   348                          ;; BEWARE OF MULTIPLY BUG! SEE https://www.c64-wiki.com/wiki/Multiply_bug
   349                          
   350                          	;; Movement
   351                          C64_MOVFM  	= $BBA2 ;Load FAC from MEM (A/Y). Returns Y=0,A=exp,fl=exp.
   352                          C64_CONUPK 	= $BA8C ;Load ARG from MEM (A/Y), set ARISIGN and A:=FAC exp.
   353                          C64_MOVAF  	= $BC0C ;Copy (rounded) FAC to ARG.
   354                          C64_MOVMF  	= $BBD4 ;Copy (rounded) FAC to MEM (X/Y). Returns A=FAC exp.
   355                          C64_MOVEF  	= $BC0F ;Copy FAC to ARG without rounding. 
   356                          C64_MOVFA  	= $BBFC ;Copy ARG to FAC
   357                          	;; Basic Arithmetic
   358                          C64_FADD   	= $B867 ;FAC := MEM (A/Y) + FAC 
   359                          C64_FADDT  	= $B86A ;FAC := ARG + FAC
   360                          C64_FSUB   	= $B850 ;FAC := MEM (A/Y) - FAC 
   361                          C64_FSUBT  	= $B853 ;FAC := ARG - FAC
   362                          C64_FMUL   	= $BA28 ;FAC := MEM (A/Y) * FAC 
   363                          C64_FMULT  	= $BA2B ;FAC := ARG * FAC
   364                          C64_MUL10  	= $BAE2 ;Quickly multiply the float held in FAC by 10.
   365                          C64_FDIV   	= $BB0F ;FAC := MEM (A/Y) / FAC, ARG := remainder.
   366                          C64_FDIVT  	= $BB12 ;FAC := ARG / FAC, ARG := remainder
   367                          C64_DIV10  	= $BAFE ;FAC := ABS(FAC / 10).
   368                          C64_NEGOP  	= $BFB4 ;Switch sign on the float in FAC, if non-zero
   369                          	;; Trigonometry
   370                          C64_SIN    	= $E26B ;Perform the SIN function on the float in FAC
   371                          C64_COS	  	= $E264 ;Perform the COS function on the float in FAC
   372                          C64_TAN    	= $E2B4 ;Perform the TAN function on the float in FAC
   373                          C64_ATN	   	= $E30E ;Perform the ATN function on the float in FAC
   374                          	;; Logarithms
   375                          C64_LOG    	= $B9EA ;FAC := ln FAC
   376                          C64_EXP	   	= $BFED ;FAC := e ^ FAC
   377                          C64_FPWR   	= $BF78 ;FAC := MEM (A/Y) ^ FAC.
   378                          C64_FPWRT  	= $BF7B ;FAC := ARG ^ FAC.
   379                          C64_SQR    	= $BF71 ;FAC := FAC ^ 0.5
   380                          C64_SQRARG 	= $BF74 ;FAC := ARG ^ 0.5
   381                          	;; Other functions
   382                          C64_FADDH  	= $B849 ;Add .5 to FAC
   383                          C64_SIGN   	= $BC2B ;Sets flags N,Z depending on whether FAC is 0, + or -.
   384                          C64_ABS	   	= $BC58 ;Perform the ABS function on the float in FAC
   385                          C64_INT    	= $BCCC ;Perform the INT function on the float in FAC
   386                          C64_FCOMP  	= $BC5B ;Compare FAC and MEM (A/Y). A = 0 (=), 1 (>), -1 (<).
   387                          	;; Type Conversion
   388                          C64_FOUT   	= $BDDD ;Convert float in FAC to a string at $100 (also in A/Y)
   389                          C64_STRVAL 	= $B7B5 ;Convert a string ($22/$23, A=length) to float in FAC.	
   390                          C64_FIN	   	= $BCF3 ;Convert a NULL-term'd string $7A/$7B to float in FAC;
   391                          			;(CHRGOT needed before, or LDA# 1st chr and CLC.)
   392                          C64_FLOATS 	= $BC44 ;Conv. 16bit signed int $62 BIG-ENDIAN to float in FAC.
   393                          C64_GIVAYF 	= $B391 ;Convert 16bit signed int (Y=lo/A=hi) to float in FAC.
   394                          C64_FLOAT  	= $BC3C ;Convert 8bit unsigned int in A to float in FAC.
   395                          C64_SNGFLT 	= $B3A2 ;Convert 8bit unsigned int in Y to float in FAC.
   396                          C64_FACINX 	= $B1AA ;Convert float in FAC to 16bit signed int (Y=lo/A=hi).
   397                          C64_QINT   	= $BC9B ;Conv. float in FAC to 32bit signed int $62 BIG-ENDIAN.
   398                          C64_FADFLT 	= $B8D2 ;Normalize value in FAC;(SEC(+)/CLC(-) needed before.)
   399                          	;; Polynomials
   400                          C64_POLY1  	= $E043 ;Evaluate a polynomial with odd powers only, for FAC
   401                          C64_POLY2  	= $E059 ;Evaluate a polynomial with odd & even powers, for FAC
   402                          	;; Constants
   403                          C64_PI     	= $AEA8 ;constant  3.14159265	= pi
   404                          C64_MININT 	= $B1A5 ;constant -32768
   405                          C64_ONE    	= $B9BC ;constant  1
   406                          C64_LOGPLY1	= $B9C2 ;constant  .434255942
   407                          C64_LOGPLY2	= $B9C7 ;constant  .576584541
   408                          C64_LOGPLY3	= $B9CC ;constant  .961800759
   409                          C64_LOGPLY4	= $B9D1 ;constant  2.88539007
   410                          C64_SQRHALF	= $B9D6 ;constant  .707106781	= sqrt 0.5
   411                          C64_SQR2   	= $B9DB ;constant  1.41421356	= sqrt 2
   412                          C64_MHALF  	= $B9E0 ;constant -.5        	= -1/2
   413                          C64_LOG2   	= $B9E5 ;constant  .693147181	= ln 2
   414                          C64_TEN    	= $BAF9 ;constant  10
   415                          C64_HMLNM01	= $BDB3 ;constant  99999999.9	(one hundred million -0.1)
   416                          C64_1BLNM1	= $BDB8 ;constant  999999999	(one billion -1)
   417                          C64_1BLN  	= $BDBD ;constant  1000000000	(one billion)
   418                          C64_HALF   	= $BF11 ;constant  .5       	= 1/2
   419                          C64_M100MLN	= $BF16 ;constant -100000000	(minus one hundred million)
   420                          C64_10MLN	= $BF1A ;constant  10000000	(ten millions)
   421                          C64_M1MLN	= $BF1E ;constant -1000000	(minus one million)
   422                          C64_100000	= $BF22 ;constant  100000
   423                          C64_M10000	= $BF26 ;constant -10000
   424                          C64_1000	= $BF2A ;constant  1000
   425                          C64_M100	= $BF2E ;constant -100
   426                          C64_10		= $BF32 ;constant  10
   427                          C64_M1		= $BF36 ;constant -1
   428                          C64_M10HOUR	= $BF3A ;constant -2160000	-10 hours (in 1/60 sec)
   429                          C64_1HOUR	= $BF3E ;constant  216000	1 hour (in 1/60 sec)
   430                          C64_M10MIN	= $BF42 ;constant -36000	-10 min (in 1/60 sec)
   431                          C64_1MIN	= $BF46 ;constant  3600		1 min (in 1/60 sec)
   432                          C64_M10SEC	= $BF4A ;constant -600		-10 sec (in 1/60 sec)
   433                          C64_1SEC	= $BF4E ;constant  60		1 sec (in 1/60 sec)
   434                          C64_LOG2M1	= $BFBF ;constant  1.44269504 	= (ln 2)^(-1)
   435                          C64_EXPPLY1	= $BFC5 ;constant  2.14987637E-05
   436                          C64_EXPPLY2	= $BFCA ;constant  1.43523140E-04
   437                          C64_EXPPLY3	= $BFCF ;constant  1.34226348E-03
   438                          C64_EXPPLY4	= $BFD4 ;constant  9.61401701E-03
   439                          C64_EXPPLY5	= $BFD9 ;constant  .0555051269
   440                          C64_EXPPLY6	= $BFDE ;constant  .240226385
   441                          C64_EXPPLY7	= $BFE3 ;constant  .693147186	= (ln 2)/2
   442                          C64_EXPPLY8	= $BFE8 ;constant  1
   443                          C64_RANDOM1	= $E08D ;constant  11879546
   444                          C64_RANDOM2	= $E092 ;constant  3.92767774E-08
   445                          C64_PIHALF 	= $E2E0 ;constant  1.57079633	= pi/2
   446                          C64_TWOPI 	= $E2E5 ;constant  6.28318531	= 2*pi
   447                          C64_QUARTER	= $E2EA ;constant  .25		= 1/4
   448                          C64_SINPLY1	= $E2F0 ;constant -14.3813907
   449                          C64_SINPLY2	= $E2F5 ;constant  42.0077971
   450                          C64_SINPLY3	= $E2FA ;constant -76.7041703

   454                          C64_ATNPLY1	= $E33F ;constant -6.84793912E-04

   508                          	;; Other useful BASIC/Kernal routines
   509                          C64_DSPP        = $EA13 ;Put char(A) and color(X) onto screen, blink delay <- 2.
   510                          C64_ERROR  	= $E38B ;Print an error string (error code in A).
   511                          C64_RESET	= $FCE2	;Reset routine, see C64_HW_RESET
   512                          	;; Input
   513                          ;C64_CHRGET	= $0073 ;in Zeropage - Get next byte of BASIC text
   514                          C64_INITAT  	= $E3A2 ;CHRGET routine in ROM
   515                          C64_GETBYTC 	= $B79B ;CHRGET uint8 to X (range checking)
   516                          C64_LINGET  	= $A96B ;CHRGET uint16 (0-63999) to $14-$15
   517                          C64_CHKOPN  	= $AEFA ;Check for and skip open paren (syntax error otherwise)
   518                          C64_CHKCLS  	= $AEF7 ;Check for and skip close paren (syntax error otherwise)
   519                          C64_CHKCOM  	= $AEFD ;Check for and skip comma (syntax error otherwise)
   520                          C64_SYNCHR  	= $AEFF ;Check for and skip char in A (syntax error otherwise)
   521                          C64_COMINT  	= $E200 ;Skip comma and get int8 in X
   522                          	;; Output
   523                          C64_STROUT  	= $AB1E ;Print zero-terminated string @ <A >Y
   524                          C64_LINPRT  	= $BDCD ;Print uint16 <X >A
   525                          C64_QPLOP   	= $A717 ;Print BASIC token from A
   526

   531                          C64_KRESTOR	= $FF8A	;JMP C64_RESTOR

   537                          C64_KMEMBOT	= $FF9C	;JMP C64_MEMBOT
   538                          C64_KSCNKEY	= $FF9F	;JMP C64_SCNKEY
   539                          C64_KSETTMO	= $FFA2	;JMP C64_SETTMO
   540                          C64_KACPTR 	= $FFA5	;JMP C64_ACPTR
   541                          C64_KCIOUT 	= $FFA8	;JMP C64_CIOUT
   542                          C64_KUNTLK 	= $FFAB	;JMP C64_UNTLK
   543                          C64_KUNLSN 	= $FFAE	;JMP C64_UNLSN
   544                          C64_KLISTEN	= $FFB1	;JMP C64_LISTEN
   545                          C64_KTALK  	= $FFB4	;JMP C64_TALK
   546                          C64_KREADST	= $FFB7	;JMP C64_READST
   547                          C64_KSETLFS	= $FFBA	;JMP C64_SETLFS
   548                          C64_KSETNAM	= $FFBD	;JMP C64_SETNAM
   549                          C64_KOPEN  	= $FFC0	;JMP(C64_IOPEN)
   550                          C64_KCLOSE 	= $FFC3	;JMP(C64_ICLOSE)

   552                          C64_KCHKOUT	= $FFC9	;JMP(C64_ICHKOUT)
   553                          C64_KCLRCHN	= $FFCC	;JMP(C64_ICLRCHN)
   554                          C64_KCHRIN 	= $FFCF	;JMP(C64_ICHRIN)

   559                          C64_KRDTIM 	= $FFDE	;JMP C64_RDTIM
   560                          C64_KSTOP  	= $FFE1	;JMP(C64_ISTOP)

   564                          C64_KSCREEN	= $FFED	;JMP C64_SCREEN

   566                          C64_KIOBASE	= $FFF3	;JMP C64_IOBASE

   574                          C64_VIC_M0X	= $D000 ;(R/W)	Sprite 0 X-pos
   575                          C64_VIC_M0Y	= $D001 ;(R/W)	Sprite 0 Y-pos

   591                          C64_VIC_CR1	= $D011 ;(R/W)	Control #1: |RST8|ECM|BMM|DEN|RSEL|YSCL2-0|

   600                          C64_VIC_IRQEN	= $D01A ;(R/W)	IRQ Enable: | ? |?|?|?|ELP|EMMC|EMBC|ERST|
   601                          C64_VIC_MDP	= $D01B ;(R/W)	Sprite/Data Priority
   602                          C64_VIC_MMC	= $D01C ;(R/W)	Sprite Multicolor mode
   603                          C64_VIC_MXE	= $D01D ;(R/W)	Sprite Horizontal Expansion
   604                          C64_VIC_MM	= $D01E ;(R/W)	Sprite to Sprite Collision Detect
   605                          C64_VIC_MD	= $D01F ;(R/W)	Sprite to Data Collision Detect
   606                          C64_VIC_EC	= $D020 ;(R/W)	Border Color
   607                          C64_VIC_B0C	= $D021 ;(R/W)	Background Color 0
   608                          C64_VIC_B1C	= $D022 ;(R/W)	Background Color 1
   609                          C64_VIC_B2C	= $D023 ;(R/W)	Background Color 2
   610                          C64_VIC_B3C	= $D024 ;(R/W)	Background Color 3
   611                          C64_VIC_MM0	= $D025 ;(R/W)	Sprite Multicolor 0
   612                          C64_VIC_MM1	= $D026 ;(R/W)	Sprite Multicolor 1
   613                          C64_VIC_M0C	= $D027 ;(R/W)	Sprite 0 Color
   614                          C64_VIC_M1C	= $D028 ;(R/W)	Sprite 1 Color
   615                          C64_VIC_M2C	= $D029 ;(R/W)	Sprite 2 Color

   109                          ;; - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - -
   110                          ;;             .__Pointer__.
   111                          ;; BLOCKS:     |_lo_0¦__hi_|            16-bit word-aligned pointers, lsb=0
   112                          ;;            /
   113                          ;; .__Header_*___Fld(0)__.___Fld(1)__._ _._Fld(Sz-1)_.
   114                          ;; |Tag_¦_Sz_|___Value___|___Value___|···|___Value___|
   115                          
   116                          	;; block test: BIT caml_is_block	sets Z=1 <=> A is a block
   117                          !addr	caml_is_block 	= .isblk + 1		;address for the above test
   118                          
   119                          	Custom_tag	= 255			;custom blocks
   120                          	Double_array_tag= 254			;arrays of unboxed floats
   121                          	Double_tag	= 253			;floats
   122                          	String_tag	= 252			;strings
   123                          	Abstract_tag	= 251			;abstracts
   124                          	No_scan_tag	= 251			;lowest tag for blks w/row data
   125                          	Forward_tag	= 250			;lazy values, forwarding ptrs
   126                          	Infix_tag	= 249			;rec. closures (must be odd!)
   127                          	Object_tag	= 248			;objects
   128                          	Closure_tag	= 247			;closures
   129                          	Lazy_tag	= 246			;lazy values
   130                          
   131                          ;; - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - -
   132                          ;; ATOMS (zero-sized blocks):
   133                          ;; .Tag_Sz_*	Atom(0) is statically allocated out of the heap, since the
   134                          ;; |_0_¦_0_|	tag=0 in blocks allocated on the heap is used by the GC to mark
   135                          ;; 		them as copied and to signal that the first field contains the
   136                          ;; forward pointer (and atoms have no fields...). Atoms with tag<>0 are not
   137                          ;; allowed, since no valid OCaml value is represented this way.
   138                          
   139                          	;; Statically allocate Atom(0)
   140                          !addr	caml_atom0 = .zeroes + 2 + .zeroes % 2	;ensure word-alignment
   141                          
   142                          ;; - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - -
   143                          ;; FLOAT blocks: C64 MFLP representation, not IEEE!
   144                          ;; .Tag_Sz_*_________._________._________.
   145                          ;; |253¦_3_|Expo¦±M_a|_n_t¦_i_s|_s_a¦_??_|	?? = unused byte
   146                          
   147                          	Double_wosize	= 3
   148                          
   149                          !macro	caml_move_float .FROM, .TO {		;Copy float block fields
   150                          	LDY # 2 * Double_wosize - 2		;and set Y=0 at exit
   151                          -	LDA (.FROM),Y
   152                          	STA (.TO),Y
   153                          	DEY
   154                          	BPL -
   155                          	INY
   156                          }
   157                          
   158                          ;; - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - -
   159                          ;; STRING blocks: length = 2 * Sz - 1 - last byte;
   160                          ;; .Tag_Sz_*___________.___________.___________.
   161                          ;; |252¦_3_|_'O'_¦_'C'_|_'a'_¦_'m'_|_'l'_¦__0__| "OCaml" length=2*3-1-0=5
   162                          ;; .Tag_Sz_*___________.___________.___________.
   163                          ;; |252¦_3_|_'C'_¦_'='_|_'6'_¦_'4'_|__0__¦__1__| "C=64"  length=2*3-1-1=4
   164                          
   165                          !macro	caml_string .str {			;Assemble the string .str
   166                          	!text .str, 0				;with proper trailing byte(s)
   167                          	!if len(.str) % 2 = 0 { !byte 1 }
   168                          }
   169                          
   170                          ;; - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - -
   171                          ;; STANDARD EXCEPTIONS:
   172                          ;; .Tag_Sz_*____________._____.
   173                          ;; |248¦_2_|_string_blk_|_oid_|			oid = -1 ... -12  = -exn_no-1
   174                          ;;          /
   175                          ;; .Tag_Sz_*_._________···__________.
   176                          ;; |252¦Sz_|_"Exception_identifier"_|
   177                          
   178                          	Out_of_memory	=  0 :	End_of_file	=  4 :	Stack_overflow	=  8
   179                          	Sys_error	=  1 :	Division_by_zero=  5 :	Sys_blocked_io	=  9
   180                          	Failure		=  2 :	Not_found	=  6 :	Assert_failure	= 10
   181                          	Invalid_argument=  3 :	Match_failure	=  7 :	Undef_rec_module= 11
   182                          	caml_exn_no	= 12
   183                          
   184                          !macro	caml_raise .exn {			;Raise a standard exception
   185                          	LDA # <caml_std_exn[.exn]
   186                          	STA ACCU
   187                          	LDA # >caml_std_exn[.exn]
   188                          	STA ACCU + 1
   189                          	JMP RAISE
   190                          }
   191                          !macro	caml_raise .exn, .str {			;As above, with a string arg.
   192                          	LDA # <.blk
   193                          	STA ACCU
   194                          	LDA # >.blk
   195                          	STA ACCU + 1
   196                          	JMP RAISE
   197                          	!align $01, $00
   198                          	!byte String_tag, (.h - .s) div 2
   199                          .s	+caml_string .str
   200                          .h	!byte 0, 2
   201                          .blk	!word caml_std_exn[.exn], .s
   202                          }
   203                          	;; Statically allocate std exns and init caml_std_exn[] ptr array
   204                          !macro	caml_alloc_exn .s, .i {
   205                          	+Val_Int ~.id, -.i - 1
   206                          	!byte String_tag, (.hdr - .str) div 2	;string block's header
   207                          .str	+caml_string .s				;string block's fields
   208                          .hdr	!byte Object_tag, 2			;exception block's header
   209                          .fld	!word .str, .id				;exception block's fields
   210                          	!set caml_std_exn = caml_std_exn + [.fld]
   211                          }
   212                          !set .x=["Out\xa4of\xa4memory","Sys\xa4error","Failure","Invalid\xa4argument"]
   213                          !set .x=.x+["End\xa4of\xa4file","Division\xa4by\xa4zero","Not\xa4found"]
   214                          !set .x=.x+["Match\xa4failure","Stack\xa4overflow","Sys\xa4blocked\xa4io"]
   215                          !set .x=.x+["Assert\xa4failure","Undef\xa4rec\xa4module"]
   216                          !set	caml_std_exn	= []			;array of pointers
   217                          !align	$01, $00
   218                          !for .i, 0, caml_exn_no - 1 { +caml_alloc_exn .x[.i], .i }

   224                          ;;
   225                          ;; RECURSIVE CLOSURE blocks: Sz = 2 * #funcs - 1 + #vars

   227                          ;; |247¦Sz_|_code0_|249¦_2_|_code1_|249¦_4_|_code2_|···|_Var0_|_Var1_|_···
   228                          ;;                  /    \___________/___\___ # of words from *0 to *i = 2i

   237                          !ifdef	caml_basic_rom_overlap {
   238                          	LDA C64_D6510				;deactivate BASIC ROM
   239                                  AND # %11111110
   240                                  STA C64_D6510

   243  098b 8e8f18             	STX caml_hw_stack_ptr			; addr. defined in runtime.asm
   244  098e a236               	LDX # caml_zp_end - caml_zp_start	;Save zeropage values so that
   245  0990 b52a               -	LDA caml_zp_start - 1,X			; they can be restored at end.
   246                          !ifdef	caml_INTERP {
   247  0992 bc630a             		LDY caml_zp_buf - 1,X		;Copy the fetch-exec routine
   248  0995 942a               		STY caml_zp_start - 1,X		; to zeropage
   249                          }

   254  099f 8d0003             	STA C64_IERROR
   255  09a2 a909               	LDA # >caml_syserr

   257  09a7 a90e               	LDA # 14				;Select upper+lowercase charset.

   264                          	;; Y is set to 0 here and, if not stated elsewhere, each routine/macro
   265                          	;; assumes Y = 0 when invoked and ensures Y = 0 stands again upon exit.
   266  09b7 a000               	LDY # 0

   281                          } else {

   291  09d6 bd630a             -	LDA caml_zp_buf - 1,X			;restore zeropage values
   292  09d9 952a               	STA caml_zp_start - 1,X
   293  09db ca                 	DEX
   294  09dc d0f8               	BNE -
   295  09de a98b               	LDA # <C64_ERROR			;restore IERROR vector
   296  09e0 8d0003             	STA C64_IERROR
   297  09e3 a9e3               	LDA # >C64_ERROR
   298  09e5 8d0103             	STA C64_IERROR + 1
   299                          !ifdef	caml_basic_rom_overlap {		;re-activate BASIC ROM
   300                                  LDA C64_D6510
   301                                  ORA # %00000011
   302                                  STA C64_D6510
   303                          }
   304  09e8 60                 	RTS					;return to BASIC prompt.
   305                          
   306                          ;; ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
   307                          ;;	RAM/ROM OVERLAP MANAGEMENT
   308                          ;; ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
   309                          
   310                          !ifndef caml_basic_rom_overlap {
   311                            !macro caml_JSR_BASROM .routine {		;call a BASIC ROM routine
   312                          	JSR .routine
   313                            }
   314                          } else {
   315                            !macro caml_JSR_BASROM .routine {		;call a BASIC ROM routine
   316                          	PHP					;save PS and A as they may be
   317                          	PHA					; arguments for the routine
   318                          	LDA # <.routine				;store the routine address at
   319                          	STA caml_basic_rom_call_addr		; wrapper's JSR argument place
   320                          	LDA # >.routine				; (see below)
   321                          	STA caml_basic_rom_call_addr + 1
   322                          	JSR caml_basic_rom_call
   323                            }
   324                          	;; assemble a wrapper here (safe place, not overlapped to ROM)
   325                          caml_basic_rom_call
   326                                  LDA C64_D6510				;activate BASIC ROM
   327                                  ORA # %00000011
   328                                  STA C64_D6510
   329                          	PLA					;restore PS and A
   330                          	PLP
   331                          caml_basic_rom_call_addr = * + 1
   332                          	JSR .dummy				;SMC: JSR routine
   333                          	PHP					;save PS and A again, as they
   334                          	PHA					; may be the routine results
   335                          	LDA C64_D6510				;deactivate BASIC ROM
   336                                  AND # %11111110
   337                                  STA C64_D6510
   338                          	PLA					;restore PS and A
   339                          	PLP
   340                          	RTS					;return to caller, in RAM
   341                          }
   342                          
   343                          ;; ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
   344                          ;;	BASIC/KERNAL ERROR HANDLER
   345                          ;; ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
   346                          
   347                          caml_syserr
   348                          !ifdef 	caml_basic_rom_overlap {
   349                          	LDA C64_D6510				;BASIC ROM might had been turned
   350                                  AND # %11111110				; on to call the routine which
   351                                  STA C64_D6510				; failed, so deactivate it
   352                          }
   353  09e9 e014               	CPX # C64_ERR_DIVISION_BY_ZERO		;If the system error matches an
   354  09eb d00b               	BNE +					; OCaml standard exception,
   355                          	+caml_raise Division_by_zero		;

; ******** Source: /home/pifu/.opam/4.14.3/lib/breadcaml/loader.asm
   362  0a44 a000               +	LDY # 0					;else raise Sys_Error(error no.)
   363  0a46 8c620a             	STY @arg
   364  0a49 8e630a             	STX @arg + 1				; save error no. using
   365  0a4c 38                 	SEC					; the OCaml int representation
   366  0a4d 2e620a             	ROL @arg
   367  0a50 2e630a             	ROL @arg + 1
   368  0a53 a960               	LDA # <@blk
   369  0a55 852c               	STA ACCU
   370  0a57 a90a               	LDA # >@blk
   371  0a59 852d               	STA ACCU + 1				; ACCU := exn address
   372  0a5b 4c8e18             	JMP RAISE				; raise Sys_error(error no.)
   373                          	!align $01, $00				;Exception block:
   374  0a5e 0002               	!byte 0, 2				; Tag, Size
   375  0a60 a408               @blk	!word caml_std_exn[Sys_error]		; Field0 = Sys_error exn
   376  0a62 3412               @arg	!word .dummy				; Field1 = Val_Int(error no.)
   377                          
   378                          ;; ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
   379                          ;; 	ZEROPAGE BUFFER - ZP values are saved here at initializazion and
   380                          ;;	restored at end. If caml_INTERP=1, the buffer also contains the
   381                          ;;	fetch-exec routine to be copied into ZP by caml_init.
   382                          ;; ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
   383                          
   384                          caml_zp_buf
   385  0a64 0000000000000000...	!fill 11				;11 by. for ZAM2 registers
   386                          !ifdef	caml_INTERP {
   387                            !pseudopc caml_interp_fetch {
   388                          		@PC  = @fetch + 1		;Bytecode interpreter
   389                          		@idx = @exec + 1		;fetch-exec routine:
   390  0a6f ad3412             @fetch		LDA .dummy			; 3 by. SMC: fetch next opc
   391  0a72 e637               		INC+1 @PC			; 2 by. incr. LDA operand (PCl)
   392  0a74 d002               		BNE +				; 2 by.
   393  0a76 e638               		INC+1 @PC + 1			; 2 by. incr. LDA operand (PCh)
   394                          +
   395                              !ifdef caml_DEBUG {
   396                          		JSR caml_debug
   397                              } else {
   398  0a78 0a                 		ASL				; 1 by. C:A = 2 * opc
   399  0a79 8543                		STA+1 @idx			; 2 by. modify JMP() operand
   400                              }
   401  0a7b 6c001e             @exec		JMP (caml_interp_jmptbl)	; 3 by. SMC: JMP(>jmptbl + A)
   402                            }						;--
   403                          } else {!fill 15}				;15 by. total
   404  0a7e 0000000000000000...	!fill 28				;28 by. for BLK, GC, TMP
   405                          
   406                          !ifdef	caml_INTERP {
   407                          !ifdef caml_DEBUG {
   408                          caml_debug
   409                          	@RevOn	= $12
   410                          	@RevOff	= $92
   411                          	@Cyan	= $9F
   412                          	@LBlue	= $9A
   413                          	@CR	= $0D
   414                          	PHP					;	[S
   415                          	PHA					;	[opc; S
   416                          	;; print a header every 22 lines
   417                          	DEC @count
   418                          	BPL +
   419                          	LDA # @CR
   420                          	JSR C64_CHROUT
   421                          	LDX # @hdr - @end			;negative number
   422                          -	LDA @end - $100,X
   423                          	JSR C64_CHROUT
   424                          	INX
   425                          	BNE -
   426                          	LDA #21
   427                          	STA @count
   428                          +	;; print PC
   429                          	LDA PC
   430                          	SEC
   431                          	SBC # 1
   432                          	PHA					;	[PC; opc; S
   433                          	LDA PC + 1
   434                          	SBC # 0
   435                          	JSR @pr_hex
   436                          	PLA					;A=PC	[opc; S
   437                          	JSR @pr_hex
   438                          	;; print opcode
   439                          	LDA # @RevOn
   440                          	JSR C64_CHROUT
   441                          	PLA					;A=opc	[S
   442                          	PHA					;	[opc; S
   443                          	JSR @pr_hex
   444                          	;; print ACCU
   445                          	LDA # @Cyan
   446                          	JSR C64_CHROUT
   447                          	LDA ACCU + 1
   448                          	JSR @pr_hex
   449                          	LDA ACCU
   450                          	JSR @pr_hex
   451                          	LDA # @RevOff
   452                          	JSR C64_CHROUT
   453                          	;; print top 6 stack elements
   454                          	LDY # 0
   455                          -	TYA
   456                          	PHA					;	[Y/4; opc; S
   457                          	LSR
   458                          	LSR
   459                          	LDA # @LBlue
   460                          	BCS +					;switch Cyan/LBlue
   461                          	LDA # @Cyan
   462                          +	JSR C64_CHROUT
   463                          	PLA					;A=Y/4	[opc; S
   464                          	TAY
   465                          	LDA (SP),Y
   466                          	TAX
   467                          	INY
   468                          	LDA (SP),Y
   469                          	JSR @pr_hex				;print STACK[i]
   470                          	TXA
   471                          	JSR @pr_hex
   472                          	INY
   473                          	CPY # 12
   474                          	BNE -
   475                          	;; print ENV
   476                          	LDA # @RevOn
   477                          	JSR C64_CHROUT
   478                          	LDA ENV + 1
   479                          	JSR @pr_hex
   480                          	LDA ENV
   481                          	JSR @pr_hex
   482                          	;; print XARGS
   483                          	LDA # @RevOff
   484                          	JSR C64_CHROUT
   485                          	LDA XARGS
   486                          	LSR
   487                          	JSR @pr_hex
   488                          	;; continue fetch-exec
   489                          	PLA					;A=opc	[S
   490                          	PLP					;S	[
   491                          	ASL
   492                          	LDY # 0
   493                          	RTS
   494                          	
   495                          @count	!byte 0
   496                          	
   497                          @pr_hex	PHA					;	[A
   498                          	LSR
   499                          	LSR
   500                          	LSR
   501                          	LSR
   502                          	LDX # 2
   503                          	BNE +
   504                          -	PLA					;A	[
   505                          	AND # $0F
   506                          +	CLC
   507                          	ADC # '0'
   508                          	CMP # '9' + 1
   509                          	BCC +
   510                          	ADC # 7 -1				;-1 as C=1
   511                          +	JSR C64_CHROUT
   512                          	DEX
   513                          	BNE -
   514                          	RTS
   515                          	
   516                          @hdr	!text @RevOn, "addr", @RevOff, "oc",   @Cyan,  "accu", @RevOn, "stk0"
   517                          	!text @LBlue, "   1", @Cyan,   "   2", @LBlue, "   3", @Cyan,  "   4"
   518                          	!text @LBlue, "   5", @RevOff, " env", @RevOn, "xa",   @RevOff
   519                          @end
   520                          
   521                          }} ;ifdef caml_INTERP & caml_DEBUG
   522                          
   524                          caml_loader_end
   525                          } ;zone caml_LOADER
